items.title: Police investigate what caused bus crash as 61yo passenger dies in hospital

children.content: A man who was critically hurt when a bus smashed into a tree in Perth's north dies from his injuries, with police yet to confirm why the driver lost control of the vehicle.

articles.title: Multiple people injured in bus crash, two critically, in Perth's north

children.content: A man and a woman suffer critical injuries and five other passengers are hurt as a Transperth bus crashes into a tree in Perth's north on Thursday morning.

image.altText: Police standing in front of a bus that is badly damaged after colliding with a tree

gallery.altText: A police officer stands in front of a bus that has crashed into a tree
Police standing in front of a bus that is badly damaged after colliding with a tree

thumbnail.altText: A police officer stands in front of a bus that has crashed into a tree

items.title: Another pandemic 'something we need to prepare for', WA's public health chief says

children.content: Clare Huppatz, the first woman to be appointed WA's chief health officer, takes up her role at a time when Ebola, diphtheria and the flu are public health concerns.

image.altText: WA Chief Health Officer Clare Huppatz speaks to a reporter

items.title: Testing underway after mass bird death in country WA

children.content: Authorities will investigate why 78 birds were found dead or dying in a wheatbelt town, with locals speculating the cause could be related to mouse baits.

image.altText: Two pink and grey galahs sit on yellow sunflowers.

items.title: Ex-teacher jailed for sexually abusing children he was babysitting

children.content: Kurt David Charlton, a former WA teacher, is sentenced for sexual offences against nine victims, who were all aged under 13 at the time.

image.altText: A man in an orange tee shirt smiles to the camera.

items.title: Unlicensed chip factory lands WA's 'spud king' in hot water

children.content: Potato baron Tony Galati's business group has been fined $20,000 after it was found to have built an illegal Spuddies potato chip factory in WA's South West.

image.altText: A supermarket owner in his store.

items.title: UN envoy intervenes in Woodside gas plant challenge

children.content: The federal government's 40-year extension of Woodside's North West Shelf gas project on the Burrup Peninsula could violate human rights, an independent United Nations expert warns.

gallery.altText: A woman smiles while giving an address at a podium.
A gas flame burns as the sun sets over Woodside's Karratha processing plant.

thumbnail.altText: A woman smiles while giving an address at a podium.
A gas flame burns as the sun sets over Woodside's Karratha processing plant.

items.title: Concerns raised as mining companies take local groundwater leaving towns 'vulnerable'

children.content: As a major miner seeks approval to extend its magnetite ore operations in regional Western Australia to 2048, shires are increasingly concerned about ensuring there's enough groundwater to go around.

image.altText: A tank protests a mining water licence application

items.title: Women say getting a long-acting reversible contraceptive has been the 'best decision'

children.content: Misinformation, cost and a lack of trained providers have stopped many Australian women from accessing what doctors consider the most effective form of reversible contraception.

image.altText: Young women leans on her kitchen bench, posing for photo

images.altText: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese in a grey suit, staring to his right, with the Australian flag behind him.

articles.title: Oil prices down almost 3pc as traders bet on Middle East peace

children.content: Brent crude has dropped almost 3 per cent overnight, while the ASX is set to trade up today. Follow the day's events and insights as they happen with our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Two women look at a share price board outside the ASX in Sydney.

articles.title: What happens when fuel excise cuts end on June 30?

children.content: Experts say consumers have 30 days until they pay more at the pump if no peace deal is finalised in the Middle East and the Strait of Hormuz remains closed.

image.altText: Five red petrol pumps in a bowser; one with a red handle, then a blue, a green, a dark blue and a black handle.

articles.title: Hanson takes the stage as budget woes roll on

children.content: While Labor is increasingly concerned about the long-term threat of One Nation, this week its attention was squarely on more immediate problems.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon and white striped tie.

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese is expected to tell a summit today that the federal government's budget measures are an attempt to push back against "economic dislocation".

items.title: Budget an attempt to combat 'economic dislocation', PM to tell summit

updates.title: Barnaby Joyce clarifies One Nation policy on forced sale of homes for migrants
Jane Hume says Coalition open to doing deal with Greens to extend inquiries
US senate committee endorses Trump's ambassador pick

items.title: ABC news quiz: Mystery markings, a premature celebration and a fight over the Big … something

children.content: You too could learn the dangers of celebrating too soon if you underestimate this week's news quiz.

image.altText: Lorena Wiebes holds up her hands

items.title: The Perth choir giving people their voice back

children.content: In an unassuming building in Perth's north, the Second Chance Choir is bringing people together through music to help them put aside years of trauma, violence and substance abuse.

gallery.altText: Close-up of a group of people singing in a choir.
A close up image of a microphone

thumbnail.altText: Close-up of a group of people singing in a choir.

items.title: The best fresh produce to shop for in June

children.content: Winter has arrived. This is some of the best seasonal produce to look for.

image.altText: A woman browsing produce shelves at the Queen Victoria Market.

items.title: Bullock says RBA will re-tender KPMG whistleblower hotline contract amid scandals

children.content: The Reserve Bank is distancing itself from KPMG with Governor Michele Bullock confirming the bank will re-tender its whistleblower hotline service.

image.altText: A dark-haired woman in glasses casts her gaze downward as she stands at a lectern in forrnt of an RBA backdrop.

items.title: Tantric workshops, couple swaps and toys: Intimacy pressures on MAFS exposed

children.content: Married At First Sight UK has become the centre of rape allegations. Now former MAFS Australia contestants and producers are speaking out about being coerced into intimacy.

image.altText: Woman sitting on a couch with a laptop on a coffee table in front of her.

items.title: Piastri hunting Monaco silverware to mark historic F1 milestone

children.content: On an historic occasion for one of Formula 1's most storied teams, Oscar Piastri is hoping to add silverware to McLaren's special day in Monaco.

image.altText: F1 driver speaking to an engineer in the garage

items.title: Murder victim tied up with plastic wrap, stomped in meth-fuelled burglary

children.content: Two men are convicted of killing 68-year-old Raymond Smith in his own home in Western Australia's South West during a meth-addled burglary for drugs.

gallery.altText: A man with glasses and his hair in a ponytail looks away from the camera.
A court sketch of a dark haired man with glasses pictured beside a blonde man
Police banner cordoning off a house with police van and equipment in front yard

items.title: Water Corp says maintenance of 54,000km of pipes a challenge amid complaints

children.content: For two years, residents in one of the driest parts of Western Australia have watched thousands of litres of water leaking from a Water Corp-owned tank.

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash

children.content: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Gold reserves grow at Kalgoorlie's Super Pit

children.content: A big exploration push by Australia's biggest gold miner, Northern Star Resources, has grown the reserves at the Super Pit by 54 per cent in the past six years.

image.altText: A mining truck carrying gold-bearing ore.

items.title: Docker O'Driscoll says mental health admission 'best thing' he's done

children.content: Fremantle Dockers midfielder Nathan O'Driscoll says he has received "nothing but love" after posting on social media about his struggles with anxiety and how he contemplated taking his own life.

image.altText: Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll celebrates a goal with a shout in front of fans at Perth Stadium, in Fremantle's white jersey.

items.title: Teen's 5-day wait in hospital emergency department prompts official apologies

children.content: The WA health minister and director of the state's country health service have unreservedly apologised to a 17-year-old left in an emergency room for 116 hours waiting for specialised mental health care.

gallery.altText: Madi sits on the foreshore in her hometown of Busselton

The exterior of a regional hospital building
